About

MEET
DAVID POLLACK

Hall of Famer. ESPN Analyst. Speaker. Author. Husband. Father.

David Pollack's story is one of relentless pursuit. Growing up in Georgia, football wasn't just a game — it was a calling. At the University of Georgia, he became one of the most decorated defenders in college football history: a three-time All-American, Lott Trophy winner, and Bednarik Award recipient. He was inducted into the College Football Hall of Fame in 2022.

After being selected in the first round of the 2005 NFL Draft by the Cincinnati Bengals, David's professional career was cut short by a career-ending neck injury. Rather than retreat, he redirected — channeling that same competitive drive into a 12-year career at ESPN as one of college football's most recognized analysts, earning seven Emmy Awards along the way.

Today, David is a sought-after keynote speaker, event emcee, and author. His message — that every single day is an opportunity to compete, grow, and give back — resonates with audiences across corporate, faith-based, and sports communities. He is also the author of Every Day Counts and the Brave Books children's title The Won't Quit Kid.

Away from the stage, David's priorities are clear: faith, family, and making every day count. He and his wife live in Georgia, where he stays connected to the community that shaped him.
